sections.content: Digital Selective Calling (DSC) is an automated digital system that allows radio operators to send pre-formatted digital messages on VHF Channel 70. DSC can alert specific stations or all stations in range, and is a core component of the Global Maritime Distress and Safety System (GMDSS).

sections.title: One-Button Distress
sections.content: DSC enables you to send a distress alert at the press of a single button. The alert includes your MMSI and, if available from a connected GPS, your position and nature of distress — transmitted automatically on Channel 70.

rows: Vessel in grave and imminent danger

sections.prompt: On which VHF channel are DSC calls transmitted?
sections.explanation: All VHF DSC calls are transmitted on Channel 70 (156.525 MHz), which is reserved exclusively for DSC digital transmissions.
